steps.title: Weigh the study pattern
steps.content: Many North American studies found a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury. Although one Finnish study did not find the correlation, several studies outside North America, including Luo and colleagues’ study in China, produced results like the North American studies, and few matched the Finnish result.

steps.title: Eliminate overclaims
steps.content: Choice A invents higher levels in Finland. Choice B says the association is particular to North America, but the outside-North-America studies weaken that hypothesis. Choice D says Luo’s study had inaccurate measurements, which is unsupported. The broader pattern suggests the two levels typically rise and fall together in fresh water. The answer is C.
